# Industrial IoT Software

> An independent guide for industrial IoT software buyers. No vendor affiliations, no sponsored placements, no consulting relationships with the platforms covered.

This site exists because most IIoT software content is produced by the vendors selling it. The operations leader evaluating a $300K platform deployment needs neutral technical ground — structured comparisons, implementation frameworks, and evaluation tools that reflect how the technology actually behaves in brownfield manufacturing environments, not how vendors position it in slide decks.

Content is written for three audiences: operations leaders (COOs, plant managers) focused on OEE and downtime outcomes; controls and OT engineers focused on protocol compatibility, edge stability, and safe integration; and IT/cloud architects focused on data architecture, pipeline scalability, and enterprise integration.

## Why This Site Exists

- [No Switzerland](/why/): The case for independent IIoT software analysis and who this site is written for

## Landscape

- [Market Overview](/landscape/): How the IIoT software market is structured by functional category — connectivity, edge, analytics, and full-stack platforms — with buyer trade-off frameworks for each
- [Vendor Index](/landscape/vendors/): Every significant IIoT software vendor organized by category (connectivity, edge compute, analytics, full-stack, historian). No rankings, no sponsored placements
- [Market Direction](/landscape/market-direction/): Where the IIoT software market is heading — the shift from closed automation stacks to open data-centric architectures and what it means for buying decisions made today
- [Protocol Reference](/landscape/protocols/): Field reference for the six protocols that move data between PLCs, sensors, historians, and cloud systems: Modbus TCP/RTU, OPC-UA, MQTT, Sparkplug B, EtherNet/IP, and PROFINET

## Comparisons

- [All Comparisons](/comparisons/): Index of all head-to-head platform comparisons, organized by category
- [Kepware vs. Litmus](/comparisons/kepware-vs-litmus/): Dedicated OPC server with 150+ protocol drivers vs. combined edge connectivity and compute platform. When protocol translation depth matters vs. when combined edge architecture is the better choice
- [Kepware vs. Ignition](/comparisons/kepware-vs-ignition/): Focused protocol translation engine vs. full industrial application platform covering SCADA, MES, and reporting. When to separate connectivity from the application layer vs. consolidate them
- [Ignition vs. Litmus](/comparisons/ignition-vs-litmus/): Full industrial application platform (Inductive Automation) vs. edge-first connectivity and compute (Litmus). Platform scope, offline operation, and fleet management compared
- [AWS IoT Greengrass vs. Azure IoT Edge](/comparisons/aws-greengrass-vs-azure-edge/): Two hyperscaler edge runtimes optimized for cloud data ingestion. Module ecosystems, local inference, offline behavior, and cloud lock-in trade-offs compared for IIoT architects
- [AVEVA PI System vs. InfluxDB](/comparisons/aveva-pi-system-vs-influxdb/): The dominant industrial historian vs. the open-source time-series challenger. Compression, query model, integration depth, and total cost compared for operations and data engineering teams
- [Siemens Insights Hub vs. Cumulocity](/comparisons/siemens-insights-hub-vs-cumulocity/): Closed Siemens automation ecosystem vs. independent open-architecture IIoT suite. When ecosystem lock-in is acceptable vs. when architectural independence is a requirement

## Guides

- [All Guides](/guides/): Index of all implementation guides for IIoT software buyers and implementers
- [IIoT Pilot Structure](/guides/iiot-pilot-structure/): How to design an IIoT proof-of-concept that survives contact with a real factory. Why most pilots fail at scale-out and how to scope the three constraints that block production deployment
- [Unified Namespace](/guides/unified-namespace/): How to structure MQTT topic hierarchies using the ISA-95 model. Practical implementation guide covering topic hierarchy design, Sparkplug B alignment, and common normalization failure modes
- [Unidirectional Data Pipeline](/guides/unidirectional-data-pipeline/): Hardware-enforced unidirectional data flow for OT/IT separation. Split-proxy architecture, forward error correction, NTP isolation, and failure modes for safety-critical manufacturing environments
- [Protocol Compatibility](/guides/protocol-compatibility/): What vendors claim vs. what actually works in the field. Firmware version conflicts, Siemens Optimized Block Access, program-scoped tags, Modbus endianness, and CIP connection limits
- [Legacy PLC Telemetry](/guides/legacy-plc-telemetry/): How to extract telemetry from older Allen-Bradley, Siemens, and Modbus RTU equipment without replacing hardware. Gateway selection, driver configuration, and data normalization for brownfield environments

## Tools

- [Vendor Comparison Tool](/tools/vendor-comparison/): Filter and compare IIoT software vendors by category, protocol support, deployment model, and pricing tier. Independent. No sponsored placements
- [ROI / Business Case Calculator](/tools/roi-calculator/): Model the financial impact of an IIoT platform deployment. Outputs payback timeline, OEE impact value, and annual cost reduction based on your operations baseline
- [PLC Bandwidth & Polling Calculator](/tools/plc-bandwidth/): Calculate network bandwidth consumed by your IIoT polling layer. Size edge gateways and validate network segments before deployment
- [UNS Topic Hierarchy Builder](/tools/uns-builder/): Design and validate a Unified Namespace MQTT topic hierarchy before broker configuration. Catches naming conflicts early and exports a specification for team and vendor review
- [IIoT Platform Evaluation Checklist](/tools/evaluation-checklist/): Score two IIoT platform finalists across six weighted evaluation categories. Identify gaps and export a scorecard for steering committee review
- [IIoT Pilot Risk Stress Tester](/tools/pilot-risk/): Assess pilot risk across seven dimensions — network readiness, OT bandwidth, integration complexity, change management — before committing scope and resources
